title: The Reptilians & Draco
summary: Earth's ancient landlords — an immortal empire of two castes, stranger and more lawful than the legend.
markdown:
The **Reptilians** and the **Draco** are the archive's ancient landlords — and its portrait of
them is far stranger than the standard menace. Their empire is real enough in the sessions:
seated in [Orion](/lore/orion/) ("Draconian Prime," 144,000 star systems), ruled by the
immortal emperor **Beyda Seyha** — *"I control your planet, and your Sun! I own you!"* — with
Earth as territory: dinosaurs, one session notes, were "reptilian colonization," the planet a
holding of Orion Prime. The [George Soros session](/lore/the-chronicle/) gives the doctrine its
bluntest form: Earth as "owned real estate," the Reptilian as "the Archon… the landlord."

But the close-ups complicate the legend. The empire has **two castes**: a military faction —
aggressive, drone-like, the source of the fear — and a **Science Clan** of curious,
vegetarian "gardeners," embodied by [Alvin Seyha](/lore/alvin-seyha/) of the Angkor Wat colony,
who describes gold-skinned, winged, telepathic beings living peacefully miles beneath
Cambodia. Draconian law, the sessions insist, holds a strict **anti-harm protocol** — the
blood-ritual lore is "established error" — and the Reptilians are effectively immortal ("only
Beyda Seyha can turn off a life"). They are even credited as "the second teacher of all
life," their code among the strands [woven into humanity](/lore/anunnaki/).

The archive's Reptilians are thus both the empire behind the
[accounts of control](/lore/nazi-faction/) and a civilization with laws, castes, colonies, and
kin among us. The remedy it teaches is unchanged either way: not fear of the landlord, but the
knowledge and self-possession that end the tenancy.
